preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] to the voiceless velar fricative [x] is a complex process of backing and spirantization. This transformation is rare as a single step but is observed in historical developments where sibilant series undergo systematic retraction into dorsal positions.

examples.note: Historical chain shift where dental affricates simplified to sibilants [s] and eventually retracted to velar [x] in the modern language (e.g., Latin 'facio' > [fatso] > [fago] stage - wait, no. Better: Latin clusters like -sc- before front vowels).

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
